title: Add section-aware Meshtastic config sync vector assertions
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-t2hn.6.2 updates meshtastic_app_compat vectors to show repeated FromRadio.config records, but the JSON sequence only records repeated config values and does not name the nine config sections or assert each section payload. What needs to be done: extend the vector generator/schema/tests so stage 69420 and legacy/full-sync vectors identify the section order (device, position, power, network, display, lora, bluetooth, security, device_ui) and validate representative payload fields for each section. Acceptance criteria: generated vectors fail if a section is missing, reordered, or encoded with the wrong oneof/payload fields; Zephyr and Python vector checks pass.
notes: Implemented section-aware Meshtastic config vector assertions without hardware. Changes: generator now has canonical CONFIG_SECTION_SPECS for device, position, power, network, display, lora, bluetooth, security, device_ui; stage 69420 and legacy/full-sync expect.config_sections name the order and include payload/field metadata; added nine standalone FromRadio.config payload vectors; schema requires config_sections for want_config_stage_69420 and legacy_full_sync_unknown_nonce and accepts config payload vectors; Python vector tests validate config_sections against independent canonical expectations and assert oneof/inner varints; Zephyr gen_vectors.py emits config rows with section enum; meshtastic_codec ztest re-encodes each section with lichen_meshtastic_encode_config_section_payload before wrapping as FromRadio.config. Local validation: PYTHONPATH=python/src python3 test/vectors/generate.py wrote 25 Meshtastic vectors; cd python && uv run pytest tests/test_vectors.py -q => 90 passed; cd python && uv run ruff check tests/test_vectors.py => passed; python3 -m py_compile test/vectors/generate.py lichen/tests/meshtastic_codec/gen_vectors.py => passed; git diff --check => passed. Note: uv run ruff check tests/test_vectors.py ../test/vectors/generate.py reports pre-existing E501 long lines throughout test/vectors/generate.py outside this slice, so scoped Ruff was limited to the changed Python test file while generator syntax/schema/tests passed. Reviews: Averroes and Locke initially found acceptance gaps (optional config_sections, generated-vs-generated Python field checks, C not exercising section encoder); fixed all; follow-up reviews from both were clean for P0/P1/P2. EC2 validation: launched i-00ea3b0b8980fe2c3 in us-east-2a with SG sg-00ad11c255ab41ad3/key lichen-t2hn622-20260702082952; attached EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 at /mnt/lichen-zephyr; ran bootstrap-host.sh and env.sh. Primary EBS worktree was behind origin/main and had untracked build/display files, so preserved it and used clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-t2hn622-validate at origin/main cd65826 plus patch. EC2 command: west twister -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_codec -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter -p native_sim --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-meshtastic-config-sections --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-t2hn622-validate/lichen => passed, 2/2 configs, 108 test cases, 0 failed, 0 errored, 0 skipped, 0 warnings, 49.14s. Size: meshtastic_codec native_sim text=75311 data=793 bss=8966; meshtastic_adapter native_sim text=122975 data=1673 bss=40510. EC2 cleanup complete: sync, unmounted /mnt/lichen-zephyr, detached EBS and confirmed volume available with no attachments, terminated instance, deleted SG and key, removed local temp key/patch/known_hosts.
close_reason: Completed: shared Meshtastic app compatibility vectors now identify the nine config sections for stage 69420 and legacy/full-sync; standalone FromRadio.config vectors assert section oneof and representative payload fields; Python/schema and Zephyr generated-vector tests consume the expanded vectors. Evidence recorded in notes, including local Python checks, two clean follow-up code reviews, EC2 native_sim Twister pass, memory sizes, and EC2 cleanup.
